title: Apply for a full Certificate of Competence to slaughter or kill animals
body: "You can use this form to apply to the Food Standards Agency (FSA) to:   get a full Certificate of Competence (CoC) under the Welfare of Animals at the Time of Killing (WATOK) regulations add operations or a type of animal to your existing full CoC    Before you apply  Read the guidance on applying for a certificate of competence to slaughter or kill animals.  To apply for a full CoC or to amend an existing full CoC, you need:   to know the activity code for the type of animal and operation you are applying for an electronic copy of your qualification certificate to prove you are qualified to perform each operation and work with each type of animal your CoC will cover your CoC ID number (the FSA would have assigned this to you when you first applied for a temporary CoC or when they issued your full CoC)   If you do not know your CoC ID number, email the FSA WATOK team: watok@food.gov.uk.  Photo for your CoC card  If you’re applying for your first full CoC, you must upload a portrait photo. Your photo must:   show your head and shoulders facing forward be taken against a plain, light-coloured background clearly show your face be between 50KB and 10MB in size be in JPEG, JPG, PNG or HEIC format   The FSA will reject your application if your face is not clearly visible in the photo.  Offences and licensing history  If you have been convicted of an animal welfare offence, you must tell us:   which law you broke what penalty you received (including any restriction or disqualification orders, with details of their start and end dates) when were you convicted of the offences   If you have been refused a licence or a CoC to kill an animal or carry out a related operation. You must tell us:   why it was refused when it was refused   If your licence or a CoC to kill an animal or carry out a related operation has been suspended or revoked (cancelled). You must tell us:   why was it suspended or revoked when was it suspended or revoked which authority suspended or revoked it   Start now   Paying for your CoC   You or your employer must arrange to pay the Food Standards Agency (FSA) once you’ve submitted your application:   £25 to issue your first CoC  £8 to amend or replace an existing CoC    The FSA accepts:   cheque debit or credit card bank transfer (BACS)   The FSA will not issue your full CoC until they receive payment.  Pay by cheque  Make your cheque payable to the Food Standards Agency and write your CoC ID number on the back.  If you’re paying for multiple Certificates of Competence (CoCs), include a list of the applicants’ names and CoC ID numbers with the cheque.  Send your cheque to:   WATOK CoC CSUFood Standards AgencyRoom 112, Foss HousePeasholme GreenYorkYO1 7PR   Pay by debit or credit card  To pay by debit or credit card, call 0330 088 0633.  Tell the operator you want to pay for a Certificate...
